file_insights.changes: Added state_manager.save_scan_timestamp(SYMBOL) calls before all 14 return statements in main()
Updated with subtask-2-4 completion details and Phase 2 summary
Updated subtask-2-4 status from pending to completed with detailed notes

patterns_discovered.description: Every exit path from main() receives timestamp tracking, ensuring scanner health visibility regardless of outcome
state_manager.save_scan_timestamp(SYMBOL) placed immediately before each return statement
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures tracking occurs even if return is unexpected

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple validation gates (volatility, news, cooldown, structure) return early with timestamp tracking

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: All exit paths must be covered
gotchas_discovered.detail: Cannot miss even one return statement or timestamp tracking becomes incomplete, defeating the purpose of scanner health monitoring
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Comprehensive diff review to verify each return has corresponding timestamp save

gotchas_discovered.detail: Same timestamp save applies to all outcomes - early error detection vs successful signal - all equally important for orchestrator visibility

approach_outcome.description: Completed subtask-2-4: Added scan timestamp tracking to main()
deliverables: 14 instrumentation points added across all return paths
Verification test created and passed (timestamp storage/retrieval confirmed)
Phase 2 of scalp_fast.py StateManager migration complete

recommendations.suggestion: Apply exhaustive return-point instrumentation pattern to other core functions (scalp_scan.py) for consistent audit logging across scanner modules
Maintain a map of all instrumented return paths per function to prevent future regressions or missed coverage during refactoring
Create integration test that validates timestamp updates across all exit conditions (error, gate, normal) to prevent silent instrumentation loss
Consider decorator pattern for timestamp tracking to reduce code duplication if this pattern spreads to multiple functions
